name: La Colada Reservoir Loop
name_original: Alcaracejos, Villaralto, Embalse de la Colada – ruta circular
description: This moderate 29.8-mile mountain biking route circles the Embalse de la Colada, offering scenic views and dehesa landscapes in Los Pedroches
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alcaracejos offers diverse terrain within the Los Pedroches region, nestled in the foothills of Sierra Morena. The landscape features significant elevations like Sierra de La Chimorra (950 meters) and Calatraveño (around 750 meters), providing challenging ascents and descents. Riders can explore extensive dehesas with holm oaks and cork oaks, as well as riparian forests along the Guadalbarbo and Cuzna rivers. Mountain olive groves also contribute to the varied scenery for mountain bike trails Alcaracejos. Best mountain bike trails around Alcaracejos The most popular mountain bike trail is La Colada Reservoir Loop, a 29.8 miles (48.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views and passes through characteristic dehesa landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Two Towers, Santa Eufemia, El Viso – circular route, a difficult 40.3 miles (64.8 km) path. This trail features significant elevation changes and panoramic views from higher ground. Local mountain bikers also love the Pozoviejo Square – Dehesa de Los Pedroches loop from Parque Periurbano San Martín, a 54.5 miles (87.7 km) trail leading through the expansive Dehesa de Los Pedroches, often completed in about 7 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Alcaracejos is defined by Sierra Morena foothills, dehesa woodlands, and riparian river valleys, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Alcaracejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alcaracejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alcaracejos?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails available around Alcaracejos, catering to various skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 400 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alcaracejos?
A: Mountain biking in Alcaracejos offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll encounter challenging ascents and descents in the foothills of Sierra Morena, including peaks like Sierra de La Chimorra (950 meters). The region is also characterized by extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; with holm oaks and cork oaks, riparian forests along the Guadalbarbo and Cuzna rivers, and unique mountain olive groves, providing varied surfaces and scenic backdrops.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Alcaracejos?
A: The trails around Alcaracejos offer a good mix of difficulty levels. There are 13 easy routes, 54 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced riders alike.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Alcaracejos?
A: Yes, Alcaracejos offers several family-friendly mountain bike trails. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' on komoot, which typically feature less elevation gain and smoother paths, suitable for a relaxed ride with children.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the trails?
A: While exploring the trails, you can discover several interesting natural features and landmarks. The region is known for its expansive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, riparian forests, and the elevated views from the Sierra de La Chimorra. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malos Pasos Spring&lt;/a&gt; or parts of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Mozarabe de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Alcaracejos?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Alcaracejos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986643625/two-towers-santa-eufemia-el-viso-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Two Towers, Santa Eufemia, El Viso – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation changes and panoramic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alcaracejos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alcaracejos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 17 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from the challenging mountain foothills to the serene &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; and river valleys, offering a rich and varied cycling experience.

Q: Can I find accommodation in Alcaracejos that is suitable for mountain bikers?
A: Yes, Alcaracejos is known to be bike-friendly. Establishments in the town center, such as the César hostel, often accommodate cyclists and provide secure storage for bikes, making it convenient for those traveling with their own equipment.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alcaracejos?
A: The diverse landscapes of Alcaracejos, including its pine forests, are particularly attractive for outdoor activities in autumn and winter. These seasons often offer cooler temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for mountain biking. However, the region's varied ecosystems provide enjoyable riding conditions throughout much of the year.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around Alcaracejos?
A: Yes, there are several long-distance mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozoviejo Square – Dehesa de Los Pedroches loop from Parque Periurbano San Martín&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 54.5-mile (87.7 km) trail that takes approximately 8 hours to complete, leading through the expansive Dehesa de Los Pedroches.

Q: What is a good moderate-difficulty route for mountain biking near Alcaracejos?
A: A popular moderate-difficulty route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988341253/la-colada-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Colada Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 29.8-mile (48.0 km) trail typically takes about 3 hours and 15 minutes to complete and offers scenic views while passing through characteristic &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscapes.
tips: Small villa in a high place. Some of the customs that are still maintained today are the jotillas danced by the olive growers in which they sing to love and shearing, a hard job that is carried out in the spring and in which the traditional scissors are preferred.
Beautiful place where you can enjoy nature and birds. It is a very popular place to go fishing - there are carp, barbel, catfish etc. It also has a barbecue area.
Nice square. This town has several establishments where we can stay. We chose the César hostel, well cared for and they let us store our bikes without any problem.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Alcaracejos, Villaralto, Embalse de la Colada mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While it requires good physical fitness due to its length and elevation gain, the technical difficulty of the terrain is manageable, making it suitable for a broad range of riders.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the route is conveniently located next to a parking area, making access straightforward for cyclists arriving by car.

faqs.question: Is the Alcaracejos, Villaralto, Embalse de la Colada route a circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, which makes it very convenient for planning a day trip as you will finish where you started.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You'll ride through a beautiful natural environment, characterized by the dehesa, an agroforestry system with oak trees and pastures typical of the Pedroches valley. A significant part of the ride offers scenic water views of the La Colada Reservoir Dam.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the charming towns of Villaralto and Alcaracejos Town Center, as well as the picturesque La Colada Reservoir Dam, which is popular for fishing.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for mountain biking during most of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding in Andalucía, allowing you to fully appreciate the natural surroundings without extreme heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Los Pedroches region?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike trail or to ride within the general Los Pedroches region.

faqs.question: Is the Alcaracejos, Villaralto, Embalse de la Colada route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife habitats. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other trail users and livestock.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Transandalus, Camino Mozárabe, and various segments of the Cañada Real de la Mesta, as well as the Camino El Viso-Villaralto.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like barbecue areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are barbecue areas available near the Embalse de la Colada (La Colada Reservoir), which can be a great spot for a break or a picnic during your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot while mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: The natural environment is home to local flora and fauna, including a variety of birds. The reservoir itself is known for fishing, with carp, barbel, and catfish, so you might spot local birdlife attracted to the water.
